reviewTexts: The location is a great place for a date and time for work! My husband and I also ordered a couple of small vegan cakes for our wedding and they were amazing as always.
awesome baked goods but really needs a quality/service check on coffee
Gluten and nut free options are 🔥 Went here around 9 PM or so. Staff friendly.
Amazing stop for a date night or after dinner. We tried the Lavender cake with honey buttercream, dark chocolate cake, and brown butter blondie with peanut butter icing. All we're excellent and rich!

What a cozy amazing cafe! Such tasty sweet treats. Not just your normal sugary affair but elevated with unique flavor and texture. You can tell a lot of time and love went into crafting those recipes. They should be proud.

Such a comfy inviting atmosphere too. A great place to meet up with a friend, have a specially coffee, and talk for hours.

And of course, what would a cafe be without their beverages? I got a hot cappuccino and it was a delight. Great bean flavor and just the right ratio of milk, froth, and espresso.

Great service, great atmosphere, great treats. The murmurings are true. Check it out.

Service is friendly...if you have 40 minutes to wait on line for a pastry. Been going here for a couple of years...they've always had issues regarding speed of service but my wife and I went two consecutive days this week. We wound up leaving both times. The lines are long and do not move.

Mediterra closes early and we needed coffee. We saw that Butterwood was open until 11pm so we thought we’d give it a try. My partner and I often share a drink because we don’t need a whole one and it’s usually enough for us. We tried to order an iced chai, but they had no ice (for an establishment open for another 7 hours) —no issue, hot is fine we said. Then they said we don’t have chai, so in a moment of quick thinking due to the limited menu options we asked for a macchiato to go. What we received was a paper espresso cup amount of coffee for $6. If I had asked for an espresso, the size wouldn’t have been so baffling, but I didn’t ask for an espresso. The young woman working the register seemed well-intentioned but clueless. She didn’t mention any sizes or that it wouldn’t be a great sharing option. I burnt my fingers holding the cup on my way out because it wasn’t double-cupped (common practice for hot coffee). My partner then burnt her tongue trying to take some of the volume off because the tiny cup was very volatile and prone to spilling as it had no lid. Not mad just disappointed and kind of get why it took me almost three years to try their coffee.

keywordPhrases.exampleQuote: The location is a great place for a date and time for work!

unfair_advantage.label: creator collaboration fit
unfair_advantage.evidence: Clear neighborhood identity in Lawrenceville (Identifies as women-owned) — a natural match for local creator audiences.
